reasoning: The US is the primary host of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, initially scheduled to host over 70 matches. While there has been significant political discord in early 2026—including travel bans and US-Iran conflict rumors that led to calls to strip the US of its hosting rights—FIFA officially clarified in March 2026 that the tournament will run as planned and it is not considering changing host cities. Furthermore, given the logistical impossibility of shifting the bulk of an expanded 48-team tournament's matches to Canada and Mexico just two months before kickoff, it is highly likely that at least *some* matches (and likely the vast majority) will be played in the US. The "any matches" wording makes the threshold for a "YES" resolution very low, cementing a high probability.
